title: Nordic‑Baltic ministers declare Russia primary long‑term threat, back Ukraine NATO bid
summary: Foreign ministers of the Nordic‑Baltic Eight meeting in Estonia label Russia the main threat to European security and call for Ukraine’s NATO accession and sustained sanctions.
contents:
## Nordic‑Baltic ministers declare Russia primary long‑term threat, back Ukraine NATO bid

### Overview
Foreign ministers of the Nordic‑Baltic Eight meeting in Estonia label Russia the main threat to European security and call for Ukraine’s NATO accession and sustained sanctions.

### Details
The ministers convened in Kuressaare to issue a joint declaration identifying Russia as the gravest and most enduring risk to Euro‑Atlantic stability, urging continued military aid to Ukraine and tighter economic pressure on Moscow, including measures targeting oil revenues and shadow maritime networks. They also push for higher European defense spending, expanded defense‑industrial capacity, and greater resilience against hybrid and information warfare, while reaffirming NATO’s central role in collective defense.

### Geographic context
Location: Kuressaare, Estonia.
